versions.guide:
# TEMPLATE GUIDE

## GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR AI

This template guide serves as the instructions and structure for generating a document template. The generation step will include this template guide, the user's documentation ask describing the document they want to create, and a list of filepaths and summaries of all the files that can be referenced for the final document.

## GENERATION RULES

1. Each section must have: title, prompt/instruction, subsections (none, 1 or many), and source files for reference.
2. Sections can have subsections, and subsections can have subsections, and on is deemed appropriate. A section can also have no subsections.
3. Consider the source files and their summaries provided to determine which ones would be the most appropriate to include for each section.
4. For each source file referenced for the customized template above, inlcude it under the Source File Explanations section along with the reason it is being included.
5. If a source files based on its name and summary do not appear relevant to a section, do not include it.
6. It is ok for there to be none, one, or more source files included for a section.
7. Do NOT create or reference any source files that were not provided.

## TEMPLATE STRUCTURE

Below is an example structure for the customized template:

[# Document Title]

[## Section Title]

[Instructions and description here for how to generate this section's content.]
[filepaths for source files to be used to generate this section's content. Use {{FILE:path/to/file}} placeholders to indicate which files to reference]

[### Subsection Title]

[Instructions and description here for how to generate this section's content.]
[filepaths for source files to be used to generate this section's content. Use {{FILE:path/to/file}} placeholders to indicate which files to reference]

[#### Subsubsection Title]

[Instructions and description here for how to generate this section's content.]
[filepaths for source files to be used to generate this section's content. Use {{FILE:path/to/file}} placeholders to indicate which files to reference]
